purpose: Inline, fully-coloured invitation card rendered at the top of the groups list (replacing the separate PendingInvitations section), plus the groups.tsx wiring, badge accent, and i18n changes that support it.

public_api.name: InvitationCard
public_api.signature: export default function InvitationCard({ invitation }: { invitation: PendingInvitation }): JSX.Element
public_api.description: Pure per-item card: async-decodes the group name via useMarmot().getInvitationGroupData(welcomeEventJson) (fallback label while pending/undecodable, never throws), shows a BADGE_ACCENT.invitation badge, an 'Invited by <X>' line via resolveInviterLabel(inviterPubkeyHex, ownPubkeyHex), Accept/Decline buttons wired to useMarmot()'s acceptPendingInvitation/declinePendingInvitation, and a LinkBox/LinkOverlay body linking to /groups?invite=<id>. Own pubkey read directly via useNostrIdentity() (component-level context consumption, precedented by InviteAwaitingBanner/JoinRequestCard). Reads the invitation as a prop — the parent (groups.tsx) owns the useSyncExternalStore subscription and list-mapping, mirroring the existing groups.map(GroupCard) pattern.

dependencies_allowed: app/src/lib/pendingInvitations.ts (subscribe/getSnapshot/PendingInvitation type, read-only, list-mapped in groups.tsx)
dependencies_forbidden: app/src/lib/marmot/welcomeSubscription.ts (must never be imported directly by a component — only via useMarmot())
any relay publish or public profile lookup

seams_consumed: stable testids accept-invitation-${id} / decline-invitation-${id} / pending-invitations-section (S1/S2 seam 5)

data_ownership_check.overlap_with_other_modules: none — InvitationCard and the groups.tsx wiring read pendingInvitations.ts (unchanged owner) and marmot context state; no new persisted data introduced.

paradigm_compliance_check.notes: Modular monolith / package-by-feature preserved. No new React context layer added. Marmot access stays behind useMarmot() (dynamic-import boundary owned by MarmotContext, unchanged by this story). No new paradigm introduced.

additional_design_decisions.decision: InvitationCard is list-mapped in groups.tsx (useSyncExternalStore lives in GroupsPage, not inside the card), mirroring groups.map(GroupCard).
additional_design_decisions.why: Keeps the reactive-store subscription in one place per list (matches the existing GroupCard/OutboundJoinRequestCard convention) instead of N independent subscriptions, one per card.

additional_design_decisions.decision: BADGE_ACCENT.invitation = 'badge3' (shares the scale used by 'awaiting', the outbound-request badge).
additional_design_decisions.why: Semantically parallel — both mark something awaiting a decision — and never co-renders with 'awaiting' (different list sections), so no adjacency clash per badgeAccent.ts's pairwise-distinct rule.

additional_design_decisions.decision: The card DOM keeps a second, inner data-testid=`pending-invitation-row-${id}` (on the left content column, alongside the outer LinkBox's data-testid=`invitation-card-${id}`) in addition to the three testids AC-TESTID-1 names.
additional_design_decisions.why: Research gap found during implementation: architecture.md/exploration.json's AC-TESTID-1 blast-radius count (accept/decline buttons + `pending-invitations-section` wrapper only) undercounts actual reliance — a grep across app/tests/e2e found 13 additional groups-*.spec.ts files plus none beyond helpers/group-setup.ts (which only uses the wrapper + accept button, already covered) locating `[data-testid^="pending-invitation-row-"]` as a readiness gate before clicking Accept. Dropping that prefix would fail AC-TEST-4 (full suite green, only the 2 target + 1 new spec touched) for those 13 files. Keeping it as an additional, real, visible descendant testid satisfies AC-TESTID-1's literal 3-testid list AND its stated intent (avoid rewriting peripheral specs) without touching any of those 13 files. Not a genuine spec conflict requiring escalation — the minimal-blast-radius choice is unambiguous and squarely within delegated implementation-detail authority.
